task_list.name: Stitch and register acquisitions
task_list.docs_info:
### Purpose

Stitches together tiles from multiple acquisitions of a plate and registers them into a single fused image per well. This task is designed for multi-cycle imaging experiments where the same well is imaged across multiple acquisitions (e.g., sequential rounds of staining).

The workflow proceeds in two steps:

1. **Stitching** — tiles within the reference acquisition are stitched together using phase cross correlation.
2. **Registration** — tiles from all other acquisitions are independently registered to the stitched reference image, correcting for shifts between cycles.

Key features:
- Uses the [`multiview_stitcher`](https://multiview-stitcher.readthedocs.io) package for stitching and registration.
- Conceptually similar to [Ashlar](https://github.com/labsyspharm/ashlar), but also supports **3D volumes** in addition to 2D.
- When `z_project` is enabled (default), stitching and registration are computed on a maximum-intensity Z-projection, and the resulting transformations are applied to the full 3D volume — reducing computation time.
- All acquisitions to process can be explicitly selected via `acquisitions_to_include`; if omitted, all acquisitions in the plate are used.
- Each acquisition can be assigned an optional **cycle name** to disambiguate channels from different rounds. If not specified, cycle names default to `cycle0`, `cycle1`, etc.

### Outputs

Creates a new OME-Zarr acquisition named **`fused`** within the same plate. For each well, the fused image contains **all channels from all registered acquisitions**, concatenated along the channel axis. Each channel is renamed with a `_{cycle_name}` suffix (e.g., `DAPI_cycle0`, `GFP_cycle1`) to distinguish channels across cycles.

If `keep_original_acquisitions` is `False`, the individual input acquisitions are removed from the plate after fusion.

### Limitations

- Each acquisition must contain a **`FOV_ROI_table`** with original stage coordinates to initialize the stitching.
- Supports only **one image per acquisition per well** — plates with multiple fields of view stored as separate images per acquisition are not supported.
- Large shifts between cycles that are not represented in the original stage coordinates are currently not handled.

optional_cycle_name.description:
Optional cycle name. Will be appended to original channel labels.
If None, defaults to `cycle{acquisition_ID}`.
optional_cycle_name.title: Optional Cycle Name

AcquisitionsSelectionModel.description: Model to select which acquisitions to process.

use_all_acquisitions.description:
If True, all acquisitions in the plate are used and `acquisitions` must
be empty.
use_all_acquisitions.title: Use All Acquisitions

acquisitions.description:
List of acquisitions to include. Only used when `use_all_acquisitions`
is False.
acquisitions.title: Acquisitions

AcquisitionsSelectionModel.title: AcquisitionsSelectionModel

mode.description: The mode to use for channel selection. Can be "label", "wavelength_id", or "index".
mode.title: Mode

identifier.description:
The identifier for the channel selection. The interpretation of this field depends
on the mode:
- If mode is "label", this should be the channel label.
- If mode is "wavelength_id", this should be the wavelength ID of the channel.
- If mode is "index", this should be the index of the channel (must be an integer).
identifier.title: Identifier

zarr_urls.title: Zarr Urls
zarr_urls.description: List of paths or urls to the individual OME-Zarr images to be processed. (Standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

zarr_dir.title: Zarr Dir
zarr_dir.description: Not used for this task. (Standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

acquisitions_to_include.title: Acquisitions To Include
acquisitions_to_include.description: Selection of acquisitions to process. If `use_all_acquisitions` is True (default), all acquisitions in the plate are used. Otherwise, only the acquisitions listed in `acquisitions` are processed.

reference_acquisition.title: Reference Acquisition
reference_acquisition.description: Acquisition to use as reference for registration.

reference_channel.title: Reference Channel
reference_channel.description: Channel to use as reference for stitching and registration.

pyramid_level.title: Pyramid Level
pyramid_level.description: Pyramid level to use for stitching and registration.

z_project.title: Z Project
z_project.description: If True, calculate stitching/registration on a z-projection and apply the calculated transformations to the full 3D image. If False, operate on the full image volume. Only used in case of 3D images.

keep_original_acquisitions.title: Keep Original Acquisitions
keep_original_acquisitions.description: If True, keep original acquisitions after registration. If False, remove them.

mode.description: The mode to use for channel selection. Can be "label", "wavelength_id", or "index".
mode.title: Mode

identifier.description:
The identifier for the channel selection. The interpretation of this field depends
on the mode:
- If mode is "label", this should be the channel label.
- If mode is "wavelength_id", this should be the wavelength ID of the channel.
- If mode is "index", this should be the index of the channel (must be an integer).
identifier.title: Identifier

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Absolute path to the new OME-Zarr image.

init_args.title: Init Args
init_args.description: Initialization arguments from the init task.
